content: I don't know what you are wanting to achieve, but in the config editor, you can change browser link color: browser.anchor_color

content: The QR code business is only for conveniently migrating your email account settings from your desktop to your phone. Since you don't have a desktop installation, use the "Add an email account now" option (see attached image.) You may need to know the correct server settings for your email provider, which can be found at their help web site, to proceed.
creator: rednoise

content:
I don't think anybody has been yet forced to move from Xfinity to Yahoo, and I read mixed messages whether or not that move will be mandatory, and least for the near future. In any case, they are supposed to give us plenty of advance warning. So, I don't believe that's your problem.

Here are my working Comcast settings. How do they compare to yours?:

Incoming Server Type: IMAP Mail Server Incoming Server Name: imap.comcast.net Port: 993 User Name: <my email address> Connection Security: SSL/TLS Authentication method: Normal Password

SMTP Server Name: smtp.comcast.net Port: 465 Connection Security: SSL/TLS Authentication method: Normal password User Name: <my email address>
creator: rednoise

content:
- go to 'account settings' pane - click the account in left sidebar - now click the 'composition&address' pane for the account - note the check box to specify whether sig goes after original content or before

Please post screenshot of what you are seeing, since you state it does not contain the above. thank you
creator: davidsk

content: Thunderbird is NOT an SMTP provider. Most Wordpress sites use plugins that provide that service. The eventual email might then be sent to an account where you use Thunderbird to access it, but your problem is within the Wordpress setup. I suggest you post your concerns on a Wordpress forum or solicit assistance from your plugin author page, assuming use of a plugin.
creator: davidsk
